etymology.description: 犬(犬) (quǎn, 'dog') + 口(口) (kǒu, 'mouth') + ⺼ (meat) -> depiction of a dog eating meat to express 'satisfied' -> modern form corrupted to include 甘 and 日
etymology.interpretation: The character originally depicted a dog consuming meat, symbolizing satisfaction or satiation. Over time, the components evolved and corrupted into their modern forms, yet retained the original concept of fulfillment or being 'satiated.'
